ip_outcome_note: Confirmed via PokemonTCG.io swsh4-16. National Pokedex no. 649 matches. Back-mounted cannon is the canonical visual identifier for Genesect — no other Pokemon shares this design element.

trivia_bullets.text: Genesect appears in Sword & Shield — Vivid Voltage (2020) as no. 016/185, a Holo Rare. It is the only Bug-type Mythical Pokemon (excluding Arceus holding an Insect Plate).

Team Plasma scientist Dudley revived Genesect from a 300-million-year-old fossil and installed the cannon on its back, aiming to create the most powerful Pokemon ever. N shut the project down; Dudley continued covertly out of spite.
sources: WebSearch: Genesect Team Plasma lore, via Bulbapedia/WebSearch snippet

trivia_bullets.text: "This Pokémon existed 300 million years ago. Team Plasma altered it and attached a cannon to its back."
Genesect has four alternate Drive forms (Shock, Burn, Chill, Douse) that change the type of Techno Blast. This card's oracle text includes Techno Blast at 120 damage with a one-turn cooldown.
sources: WebSearch: Genesect Drive forms, Bulbapedia snippet

trivia_bullets.text: Genesect starred in Pokemon the Movie: Genesect and the Legend Awakened (2013 Japan / 2014 US). Five Genesect led by a Shiny Red Genesect attempt to reclaim territory that no longer exists after 300 million years. Mewtwo (a distinct female-voiced individual, later identified as Mega Mewtwo Y) confronts them.
sources: Wikipedia: Pokemon the Movie: Genesect and the Legend Awakened
